_doc: Country-agnostic emissions->deaths dose-response for the health channel. See docs/design/emissions-to-health-dose-response.md.
_method: deaths_change = total_ambient_pm25_deaths[country] (the country's GBD export) * M[country] * emissions_change_fraction (CLEWS sector delta); M = energy_mass_share * crf_elasticity
_full_table: ogclews_link/data/mcduffie2021_pm25_source_shares.csv -- verbatim mirror of McDuffie Supp Data 1 (regions + 204 countries + sub-national); energy_total_pct = energy_coal + energy_noncoal. Use it to add any future country.

_sources.energy_mass_share: McDuffie et al. 2021, Nature Communications 12:3594 (s41467-021-23853-y), Supplementary Data 1 (2017): per-country % of population-weighted PM2.5 MASS from the energy/power sector = 'Energy Coal' + 'Energy NonCoal'. CC-BY.
_sources.crf_elasticity: GBD MR-BRT/IER + GEMM (Burnett et al. 2018, PNAS 115(38):9592); concave, dln(deaths)/dln(conc) ~0.78-0.86 over 15-40 ug/m3 (declining with exposure)
_sources.total_deaths: the country's own GBD ambient-PM2.5 export (IHME-GBD_2023_DATA) -- NOT McDuffie. PHL = 43,951 (2023).

Philippines.note: M ~= 0.082 -> -7.7% power emissions => ~-0.63% of total ambient deaths

Indonesia.note: energy mostly coal; residential dominates total PM2.5

South Africa.share_source: McDuffie 2021 Supp Data 1 (firm); energy-coal 20.5% = largest single source
South Africa.note: M ~2x the others -- coal-heavy power; the health channel matters most here

Ethiopia.note: energy almost all non-coal; higher exposure -> lower elasticity

_crf_elasticity_note: Energy MASS shares are now firm per-country (McDuffie). The remaining approximation is the CRF elasticity: taken from the concave GBD/GEMM curve at each country's exposure (~0.84 near 18 ug/m3 down to ~0.76 near 33 ug/m3); recompute from GEMM/IER parameters when precision matters. The SHARE is the dominant, now-resolved term.

_future_household_air_pollution._what: SEPARATE GBD risk factor: 'Household air pollution from solid fuels' (INDOOR exposure). DISTINCT from ambient PM2.5 above.
_future_household_air_pollution._for: a future household-fuel-change channel (biomass/charcoal cooking -> clean fuels/electric)
_future_household_air_pollution._use: GBD HAP rei attributable deaths (a separate IHME export; our current GBD file is AMBIENT only) + % population using solid fuels + the HAP CRF -- NOT the ambient residential share.
_future_household_air_pollution._why_distinct: McDuffie 'residential' = residential combustion's contribution to OUTDOOR ambient PM2.5; HAP = INDOOR exposure. Largest burden in low-income / high-solid-fuel-use settings (Ethiopia, rural Indonesia/Philippines).
